subject: Public Health
title: Antigens and Antibodies
description: A micro-lesson that makes Antigens and Antibodies usable.

hook.text: Every virus and bacterium wears a unique molecular 'uniform'—and your immune system learns to spot it.

buildup.text: Antigens are molecular markers on pathogens. Antibodies are Y-shaped proteins that bind to them precisely.

discovery.text: Each antibody fits one antigen like a key fits a lock—your body produces billions of unique antibodies.

twist.text: Vaccines present antigens without the disease—tricking the body into making antibodies before infection strikes.

climax.text: These pre-made antibodies neutralize real pathogens on contact—stopping infection before symptoms begin.

punchline.text: Your body builds billions of molecular locks. Vaccines test the right keys.

quiz.question: What is the relationship between antigens and antibodies?
options: Antibodies bind specifically to antigens like a key fits a lock
